DISCOVERY 301, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFF'S INTERROGATORIES, SET ONE. ARGUED AND THE COURT RULES AS FOLLOWS: AS TO FORM INTERROGATORY 209.2, DENIED AS TO CIVIL RIGHTS ACTIONS AND LIMITED TO ALL TITLE 7 AND FEHA CLAIMS ARISING OUT OF THE BRIDGE DIVISION. COUNSEL TO SUBMIT A STIPULATED ORDER. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, NOT REPORTED

LAW AND MOTION 301, PLAINTIFF JESSICA BLACK'S MOTION TO COMPEL SITE INSPECTION OF DEFENDANT'S PREMISES. PLAINTIFF JESSICA BLACK'S MOTION TO COMPEL SITE INSPECTION OF DEFENDANT'S PREMISES, AND FOR SANCTIONS, IS GRANTED IN PART, DENIED IN PART, AND CONTINUED FOR FURTHER HEARING TO MAY 27, 2026. BOTH PARTIES SEEK SANCTIONS. THE COURT ORDERS THE DISTRICT TO PAY $500 IN SANCTIONS TO BLACK WITHIN 30 DAYS. (SEE WORD MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING) COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (301/CVA)

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Site Inspection Of Defendant'S Premises
Off Calendar May-27-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL SITE INSPECTION OF DEFENDANT'S PREMISES

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff Jessica Black's motion to compel site inspection of defendant's premises is granted in part and denied in part.

By minute order on May 4, 2026, the court granted Black's motion in part, making the following findings:

The court makes a finding that her request for site inspection is proper and should be permitted as to the lobby/waiting area, the reception area, the break and/or lunch room, the main/center work area, the support staff cubicles, the Deputy General Manager's Office, Captain Elauria's Office, Sergeant Roth's workspace, the sergeant's office and main command center, the dispatch office, the administrative building boardroom, and the administrative building committee room. With respect to the men's and women's locker rooms, the District has sustained its burden of justifying its relevance objection; the District presents evidence that these rooms have been significantly renovated. With respect to Black's catchall request to inspect "any and all areas Plaintiff worked during her employment with Defendant," the court denies relief.

The court further ordered the parties to meet and confer about the terms of inspection for the sites where the court found inspection was proper. The court also ordered sanctions against defendant.

The parties have submitted supplemental briefs following their meet and confer efforts. The items the parties identify as remaining in dispute are as follows:

1. Plaintiff identifies a dispute about photographing or recording the Men's Locker Room.
2. Defendant agrees that photographing or recording the Men's Locker Room is in dispute, and asks the court to reconsider its prior sanctions order.

The court denies further relief to both parties. The court's prior order did not order inspection of the locker rooms, and no party contested the court's prior order. The court's prior order compelled sanctions, and no party contested. The parties shall adhere to the agreement they reached about inspections, and defendant shall pay the ordered sanctions within 30 days.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Christine Van Aken, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Site Inspection Of Defendant'S Premises
Off Calendar May-04-2026 Continued To May-27-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 301
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF JESSICA BLACK's Motion To Compel Site Inspection Of Defendant's Premises.

Plaintiff Jessica Black's motion to compel site inspection of defendant's premises, and for sanctions, is granted in part, denied in part, and continued for further hearing to May 27, 2026. Both parties seek sanctions. The court orders the District to pay $500 in sanctions to Black within 30 days.

(SEE WORD MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One With Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-06-2026 Continued To Mar-17-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 301
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF JESSICA BLACK'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO PLAINTIFF'S REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET ONE WITH SANCTIONS.

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Continued to March 17, 2026 to give the parties an opportunity to meet and confer in an effort to resolve all or some of the issues raised by the motion. The parties are required to fully meet and confer as to each issue raised by the motion no later than 6pm on March 12, 2026 in person or by video platform. As non-binding guidance for the parties, the court states that: 1) stipulated protective orders are generally a good way to resolve discovery disputes regarding assertedly sensitive and confidential information; 2) a privilege log is usually required for responsive information withheld on the grounds of privilege; 3) in employment cases general form interrogatories are often of little or no value and it is better to use employment form interrogatories; 4) discovery of insurance information is not unlimited, but is usually tied to what will assist the parties in engaging in settlement efforts; and 5) discovery of "me too" information is not unlimited, but is usually tied to the same or similar circumstances, people involved, and locations as as the basis for plaintiff's claims. Counsel for plaintiff Jessica Black is required to send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org no later than 4pm on March 16, 2026 stating, without argument, whether all issues raised by the motion have been resolved by the parties and, if not, identifying what issues need to be resolved by the court. Visiting Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (301/HEK)
